_label: CACHED / LABELED ANALYSIS OF REAL APPLE RSS — offline review only
source_note: Validated DeepSeek analysis generated from the matching real Apple RSS snapshot. Does not replace live analysis for new inputs.

conflicting_themes: Some users still acknowledge the app's workout quality despite frustrations with monetization.
data_limitations: Sample size of 40 reviews may not capture all issues; 500 total reviews available.
Some reviews are short and lack detail, limiting depth of analysis.

anti_hallucination: Require source_review_ids for every finding/requirement/test case
JSON schema prompts forbid inventing review IDs
Post-validation drops unsupported IDs and marks assumptions
Evidence sufficiency gate when sample_count is low
model_meta.failure_handling: On API failure, workflow logs error; if ALLOW_CACHED_FALLBACK and demo app cache exists, load labeled cache; else heuristic fallback.

findings.title: Misleading free trial and billing issues
findings.uncertainty: Low; clear pattern of billing complaints.

prd.prd_markdown:
# PRD: Fitness App v1.1-v1.4

## Background
Based on analysis of 500 reviews, key pain points include billing fraud (free trial charges), loss of free features, aggressive ads, login issues, poor support, and repetitive content. Immediate action needed to stop user churn and revenue loss.

## Goals
1. Eliminate billing trust issues (P0)
2. Restore free tier value and transparency (P0)
3. Fix ad experience and login stability (P0-P1)
4. Improve support and content quality (P1-P2)

## Requirements
See JSON above for full list of 10 requirements.

## Version Plan
| Version | Theme | Requirements |
|---------|-------|--------------|
| v1.1 | Billing & Trust Fix | REQ-1, REQ-2, REQ-3 |
| v1.2 | Free Tier & Onboarding | REQ-4, REQ-5, REQ-6 |
| v1.3 | Ads & Login | REQ-7, REQ-8 |
| v1.4 | Support & Content | REQ-9, REQ-10 |

## Risks
- Grandfathering free features may reduce subscription conversions short-term.
- Ad fix may impact ad revenue; need to balance with user experience.
- Support fix requires backend changes; may need additional resources.

## Open Questions
- Should we offer refunds for users charged incorrectly during trial? (Legal/financial impact)
- What is the exact date for grandfathering saved workouts? (Need to define cutoff)
- How to handle users who already paid for a year but cannot log in? (Manual intervention needed)

acceptance_criteria: Users who start a free trial are not charged until the trial end date.
The sign-up screen shows: 'Free trial until [date], then $[amount]/[period]'.
No charge appears on user's payment method until after trial expires.
If user cancels during trial, no charge occurs.
requirements.description: Ensure that for any subscription with a free trial, the first charge occurs only after the trial period expires. The app must clearly display the trial duration and the exact date of first charge at sign-up. No charges should be processed until the trial is over.
out_of_scope: Changing subscription plans or pricing tiers
requirements.title: Fix free trial billing to charge only after trial ends
requirements.user_problem: Users sign up for a free trial but are charged immediately or before the trial period ends, leading to unexpected charges.

acceptance_criteria: Cancellation is processed within 1 minute of user action.
No additional charges appear after cancellation effective date.
User receives in-app and email confirmation of cancellation.
If cancellation occurs mid-cycle, access continues until end of current period but no renewal charge.
requirements.description: When a user cancels their subscription via the app or platform (iOS/Android), the cancellation must be processed immediately. No further billing cycles should occur. The user should receive a confirmation of cancellation with the effective date and confirmation that no further charges will be made.
requirements.title: Process cancellations immediately and stop further charges
requirements.user_problem: Users who cancel their subscription continue to be charged, sometimes multiple times, indicating a failure in cancellation processing.

acceptance_criteria: Subscription settings show plan name, price, renewal date.
Billing history lists last 12 months of charges with plan details.
Users can see the exact amount charged and for which period.
requirements.description: Add a 'Billing History' section in the subscription settings that shows all past charges, the plan name, amount, date, and next billing date. The current plan details should be prominently displayed.
requirements.title: Add billing history and transparency in subscription settings
requirements.user_problem: Users are confused about what plan they are on and why they were charged a specific amount.

acceptance_criteria: Free tier includes at least 10 permanent workouts, basic progress tracking, and daily tip.
App store description and in-app onboarding clearly list what is free vs paid.
No feature that was free before v1.1 is removed from free tier without 3-month notice.
Users who previously had free access to a feature retain that access (grandfathering).
requirements.description: Define a clear permanent free tier that includes a set of core workouts and features that will never require payment. Communicate this transparently on the app store listing and in the app. The free tier should not be 'trial-like' but a stable offering.
requirements.title: Define and communicate a clear free tier with permanent features
requirements.user_problem: Long-time users relied on features that were free and then became paywalled. New users discover a free trial that drastically restricts access after a short period.

acceptance_criteria: User can access any workout saved before v1.1 release without subscription.
New saved content after v1.1 follows the free tier rules.
No paywall prompt appears for grandfathered content.
requirements.description: Any workout, plan, or content that was saved by a user while it was free must remain accessible to that user without a subscription. The system should check if the content was saved before a certain date (e.g., release date of v1.1) and grant free access.
out_of_scope: Restoring content that was never saved
requirements.title: Remove paywall from previously saved user content
requirements.user_problem: Users who saved workouts or plans when they were free now find them locked behind a paywall, causing frustration.

acceptance_criteria: Onboarding offers at least 5 goal options including 'General Fitness' and 'No Goal'.
Weight loss is not the first or most highlighted option.
User can skip goal setting and still access main app features.
requirements.description: Redesign onboarding flow to include multiple goal options (e.g., strength, flexibility, general fitness) and allow users to skip goal setting entirely. Weight loss should not be the default or only prominent option.
out_of_scope: Personalized workout plans based on goals
requirements.title: Revise onboarding to reduce weight loss emphasis and allow skip
requirements.user_problem: Onboarding strongly emphasizes weight loss goals, making users feel pressured or uncomfortable. Also, users cannot use the app without setting a weight loss goal.

acceptance_criteria: No ad opens external browser or app without user tapping an ad element.
After a 90-second ad, the workout screen loads within 3 seconds.
If ad fails to load, user can skip after 5 seconds.
Ad frequency: max one 90-second ad per workout session (not per exercise).
requirements.description: Ensure that ads do not trigger external app or website openings without explicit user tap. Also, fix the ad loading flow so that after the ad completes, the workout resumes reliably. If an ad fails to load, it should be skippable after 5 seconds or auto-dismissed.
out_of_scope: Removing ads entirely for free tier
requirements.title: Fix ads to not open external apps or stall workouts
requirements.user_problem: Ads open external websites (e.g., SHEIN) without user interaction, and 90-second ads sometimes cause the app to not load, breaking the workout experience.

acceptance_criteria: User can log in with email/password and social login without errors.
After login, paid subscription is recognized and features unlocked.
User can log in on another device and access subscription.
Restore Purchases button works and restores subscription within 10 seconds.
requirements.description: Investigate and fix the authentication flow to ensure that paying users can log in reliably. Subscriptions should be tied to the user account and accessible on all devices where the user logs in. Implement a 'Restore Purchases' button for iOS and Android.
requirements.title: Fix login and multi-device subscription access
requirements.user_problem: Users cannot log in after paying, or are logged out and cannot access their subscription. Also, subscription is not accessible on multiple devices.

acceptance_criteria: Support form accepts all submissions without false spam flag.
User receives confirmation (in-app or email) that their inquiry was received.
Support response within 48 hours.
requirements.description: Review and fix the spam detection logic for the support contact form. Ensure legitimate user inquiries are not blocked. Provide alternative contact methods (email, in-app chat) and confirm receipt of the message.
requirements.title: Fix customer support contact form to not flag as spam
requirements.user_problem: Users trying to contact support via the app's form get a 'spam' error and cannot submit their inquiry.

acceptance_criteria: No workout repeats within 30 days for free users, 60 days for paid.
At least 5 new workouts added per month from human curation.
AI-generated content is reviewed by a human before publication.
requirements.description: Introduce a content rotation algorithm that ensures workouts are not repeated within a 30-day period for free users and 60-day for paid users. Add new content from human trainers periodically.
requirements.title: Improve content variety and reduce repetition
requirements.user_problem: Users find daily workouts repetitive and 'AI slop', lacking variety.

version_plan.goal: Resolve misleading free trial, billing errors, and cancellation issues to stop immediate financial harm and restore trust.
Improve free tier transparency, reduce paywall frustration, and fix onboarding to set correct expectations.
Fix aggressive ads that break the app and resolve login/access issues for paid users.
Revamp support contact flow and improve content quality/relevance.

test_cases.expected_result: Trial end date shown; Apple manage sheet opens; copy shows price/period clearly
steps: Create sandbox account entering last 48h of trial
Open app home and paywall surfaces
test_cases.title: Trial-end reminder visible before charge

test_cases.expected_result: No crash; draft restored; final save succeeds
steps: Enable airplane mode and tap save
Force quit, relaunch, restore draft, save online
test_cases.title: Save workout does not crash; draft recovers

steps: Finish and start a new session next day
test_cases.title: Custom rest timer persists

test_cases.expected_result: Quality steps down once; session remains usable
steps: Play trainer video until buffer stall
test_cases.title: Buffer triggers quality step-down

validation.summary: Validation complete: 7 findings, 10 requirements, 4 test cases. Issues logged: 6; revisions applied: 5.

scope_note: No goal provided; using all unique reviews.

method_choices.semantic: LLM JSON topic discovery + PRD + tests when configured
